title: Carpenter Ant Control in New Brunswick, NJ | $350 – $2,000 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional carpenter ant control in New Brunswick, NJ. Protect your home from wood-damaging carpenter ants. Expert treatment $350 – $2,000. Same-day service available!
intro: New Brunswick residents face unique carpenter ant challenges due to the city's mix of historic Victorian homes, college housing, and modern developments along the Raritan River. These wood-destroying pests thrive in New Brunswick's humid climate and older wooden structures, causing significant structural damage if left untreated. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive carpenter ant elimination services throughout New Brunswick, from the Rutgers campus area to the French Street neighborhood.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over 15 years of specialized experience treating carpenter ant infestations in New Brunswick's diverse residential and commercial properties. Our licensed technicians use advanced detection methods and eco-friendly treatments that are safe for families, pets, and the environment while delivering guaranteed results.
localContext: New Brunswick's century-old brownstones, Dutch colonial homes near Buccleuch Park, and multi-family dwellings along George Street are particularly vulnerable to carpenter ant damage due to aging wood structures and moisture issues. The city's proximity to the Raritan River and Delaware and Raritan Canal creates ideal humid conditions that attract carpenter ants seeking damp wood to colonize.

faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in New Brunswick, NJ?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in New Brunswick typically ranges from $350 to $2,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, size of your property, and number of colonies present. Smaller single-colony treatments start around $350, while extensive infestations in larger homes or multi-unit buildings can reach $2,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before any treatment begins.

faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in New Brunswick?
faqs.answer: New Brunswick's combination of older wooden structures, high humidity from the Raritan River, and mature tree canopy creates ideal conditions for carpenter ants. Many of the city's historic homes have moisture issues in basements and crawl spaces, and the abundance of older trees provides natural nesting sites from which ants migrate indoors. The dense urban environment also means colonies can easily spread between adjacent properties.

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants, typically ½ to ⅝ inch long, and are usually black or dark brown. Key signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) that look like sawdust near wooden structures, faint rustling sounds in walls, and winged ants emerging in spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests, leaving behind smooth tunnels and galleries.

faqs.question: How quickly can you treat my New Brunswick home for carpenter ants?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service for New Brunswick residents in most cases. After your initial inspection, treatment typically begins within 24-48 hours. The treatment process itself usually takes 2-4 hours depending on your property size and infestation severity. Most infestations are eliminated within 2-3 weeks, with follow-up visits scheduled to ensure complete eradication.
